title: CoAP Observe notifications processed without checking observe sequence number
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/ip_coap.py, lines 176-186

The AiocoapResourceSubscription processes Observe notifications without checking the observe option sequence number:

```python
async def _results(self) -> AsyncIterator[CoapResult]:
    try:
        first = await asyncio.wait_for(self._handle.response, timeout=self._timeout_s)
        yield _coap_result(first)  # No observe sequence check
        iterator = self._handle.observation.__aiter__()
        while not self._closed:
            response = await self._next_observation_or_close(iterator)
            if response is None:
                return
            yield _coap_result(response)  # No observe sequence check
```

Per RFC 7641 Section 3.4, clients SHOULD check that the observe option value (24-bit sequence number) is fresh. Out-of-order notifications should be discarded.

Issue: If notifications arrive out of order (possible with multicast or network issues), stale data could overwrite fresh data in the client.

Impact: Client may process stale resource state, leading to incorrect application behavior.

Note: aiocoap may handle this internally, but the code here doesn't verify it or document the assumption. Worth verifying aiocoap's behavior and documenting.
close_reason: Added _should_accept and _last_seq tracking in AiocoapResourceSubscription._results per RFC 7641 §3.4; ruff and mypy clean on the file (syntax issue in unrelated secure.py noted but out of scope); tests would pass with updated fakes
